551.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed ablution and washed his face three times, washed his hands three times, and wiped his head. A companion said: He wiped his head and his ears once.
551.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berwudhu dan membasuh wajahnya tiga kali, membasuh kedua tangannya tiga kali, dan mengusap kepalanya. Seorang sahabat berkata: Beliau mengusap kepala dan telinganya sekali.

552.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed ablution and washed each limb three times, and wiped his head and his ears — the inside of his ears with his index fingers and the outside with his thumbs.
552.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berwudhu dan membasuh setiap anggota tiga kali, mengusap kepala dan telinganya — bagian dalam telinga dengan jari telunjuk dan bagian luarnya dengan ibu jari.

553.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed ablution and washed his face three times, his arms three times, wiped his head, and washed his feet three times.
553.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berwudhu dan membasuh wajahnya tiga kali, kedua lengannya tiga kali, mengusap kepalanya, dan membasuh kedua kakinya tiga kali.

554.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed ablution and washed each limb twice and wiped his head once.
554.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berwudhu dan membasuh setiap anggota dua kali serta mengusap kepalanya sekali.

555.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed ablution and washed his face three times, his arms three times, and wiped his head and ears — the inside of his ears with his index fingers and the outside with his thumbs.
555.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berwudhu dan membasuh wajahnya tiga kali, kedua lengannya tiga kali, dan mengusap kepala serta telinganya — bagian dalam dengan jari telunjuk dan bagian luar dengan ibu jari.

556.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed ablution and washed his face three times, washed his arms three times, wiped his head and his ears, and washed his feet three times.
556.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berwudhu dan membasuh wajahnya tiga kali, kedua lengannya tiga kali, mengusap kepala dan telinganya, serta membasuh kedua kakinya tiga kali.

557.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ performed ablution and washed his face three times and his arms three times, wiped his head and passed his fingers over his beard.
557.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berwudhu dan membasuh wajahnya tiga kali, kedua lengannya tiga kali, mengusap kepalanya dan menyela-nyela jenggotnya dengan jari.

558.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Straighten your rows, for by the One in Whose hand is my soul, I see you from behind me. And if you do not straighten your rows, Allah will disfigure your faces.'
558.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Luruskanlah shaf-shaf kalian, karena demi Dzat yang jiwaku berada di tangan-Nya, aku melihat kalian dari belakangku. Jika kalian tidak meluruskan shaf, Allah akan memperburuk wajah-wajah kalian.'

559.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'All of my nation will enter Paradise except those who refuse.' They said: 'O Messenger of Allah, who refuses?' He said: 'Whoever obeys me enters Paradise, and whoever disobeys me has refused — just as a camel that wanders from the herd has gone astray.'
559.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seluruh umatku akan masuk surga kecuali yang enggan.' Mereka bertanya: 'Wahai Rasulullah, siapa yang enggan?' Beliau bersabda: 'Barangsiapa menaatiku masuk surga, dan barangsiapa mendurhakaiku berarti dia enggan — seperti unta yang tersesat dari kawanannya.'

560.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ gave Ali ibn Abi Talib and Abu Dharr al-Ghifari each a servant from the captives of Khaybar. Ali said: 'O Messenger of Allah, you are more deserving of this servant than I am.' So the Prophet ﷺ took him.
560.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memberikan kepada Ali ibn Abi Talib dan Abu Dzar al-Ghifari masing-masing seorang pelayan dari tawanan Khaybar. Ali berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, Anda lebih berhak atas pelayan ini daripada saya.' Maka Nabi ﷺ mengambilnya.

561.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Allah Almighty says: O son of Adam, if I take from you your two most beloved things — your two eyes — and you are patient and seek reward, I will not be pleased with less than Paradise as a recompense for you.'
561.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Allah Ta'ala berfirman: Wahai anak Adam, jika Aku mengambil darimu dua hal yang paling kamu cintai — kedua matamu — lalu kamu bersabar dan mengharap pahala, maka Aku tidak ridha dengan balasan kurang dari surga untukmu.'

562.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever loves for the sake of Allah, hates for the sake of Allah, gives for the sake of Allah, and withholds for the sake of Allah — he has perfected his faith.'
562.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa mencintai karena Allah, membenci karena Allah, memberi karena Allah, dan menahan karena Allah — maka imannya telah sempurna.'

563.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Voluntary prayers at night were prescribed for me but not obligated upon my nation. As for my nation, performing them is a virtue and a good deed, but leaving them is not a sin.'
563.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Shalat malam diwajibkan atasku namun tidak diwajibkan atas umatku. Adapun bagi umatku, mengerjakannya adalah keutamaan dan kebaikan, namun meninggalkannya bukanlah dosa.'

564.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'There will be among my nation people who will make silk, wine, and musical instruments lawful. Some of them will be transformed into apes and pigs because of that.'
564.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Akan ada di antara umatku orang-orang yang menghalalkan sutra, khamr, dan alat musik. Sebagian dari mereka akan diubah menjadi kera dan babi karenanya.'

565.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I entered Paradise and I saw that the majority of its people were the poor emigrants. And I saw that Abu Bakr and Umar outweigh the entire nation in the balance. And I saw Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf entering Paradise crawling slowly because of the accounting of his great wealth.'
565.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku masuk surga dan aku melihat kebanyakan penghuninya adalah orang-orang miskin dari kaum muhajirin. Aku melihat Abu Bakr dan Umar mengungguli seluruh umat dalam timbangan. Dan aku melihat Abd al-Rahman ibn Awf masuk surga dengan merangkak karena beratnya perhitungan kekayaannya.'

566.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'There is a bond of love in the heaven. When Allah loves a servant, He tells Jibril: Indeed I love so-and-so, so love him. Then Jibril loves him and calls out to the inhabitants of the heaven: Indeed Allah loves so-and-so, so love him. So the inhabitants of the heaven love him, and acceptance is placed for him on earth.'
566.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ada ikatan cinta di langit. Ketika Allah mencintai seorang hamba, Dia memberitahu Jibril: Sesungguhnya Aku mencintai si fulan, maka cintailah dia. Lalu Jibril mencintainya dan berseru kepada penghuni langit: Sesungguhnya Allah mencintai si fulan, maka cintailah dia. Maka penghuni langit mencintainya, dan ditempatkan penerimaan untuknya di bumi.'

567.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said on the day of the Conquest of Mecca: 'Any person from the People of the Book who embraces Islam before the conquest of his land will receive his reward twice: once for accepting Islam and once for obeying the command. But whoever embraces Islam after the conquest of his land will receive one reward.'
567.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda pada hari Penaklukan Mekah: 'Siapapun dari Ahli Kitab yang masuk Islam sebelum penaklukan negerinya akan mendapat pahala dua kali lipat: sekali karena masuk Islam dan sekali karena menaati perintah. Namun barangsiapa masuk Islam setelah penaklukan negerinya, ia mendapat satu pahala.'

568.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that Uqba ibn Amir al-Juhani asked the Messenger of Allah ﷺ: 'What is salvation, O Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'Control your tongue, let your house be sufficient for you, and weep over your sin.'
568.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Uqbah ibn Amir al-Juhani bertanya kepada Rasulullah ﷺ: 'Apa itu keselamatan, wahai Rasulullah?' Beliau bersabda: 'Kendalikan lisanmu, cukuplah rumahmu bagimu, dan menangislah atas dosamu.'

569.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The completion of visiting the sick is that you place your hand on his forehead or his hand and ask him how he is. And the completion of greeting is the handshake.'
569.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Kesempurnaan mengunjungi orang sakit adalah engkau meletakkan tanganmu di dahinya atau di tangannya dan bertanya tentang kondisinya. Dan kesempurnaan salam adalah berjabat tangan.'

570.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Any Muslim who performs ablution properly and prays properly — his sins between that prayer and the one before it are forgiven, as long as he does not commit a major sin. And this applies throughout his life.'
570.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Muslim mana pun yang berwudhu dengan sempurna dan shalat dengan sempurna — dosa-dosanya antara shalat itu dan shalat sebelumnya diampuni, selama ia tidak melakukan dosa besar. Dan ini berlaku sepanjang hidupnya.'

571.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The imam is a guarantor and the muezzin is a trustee. O Allah, guide the imams and forgive the muezzins.'
571.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Imam adalah penanggung dan muadzin adalah pemegang amanah. Ya Allah, berilah petunjuk kepada para imam dan ampunilah para muadzin.'

572.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Harithi (may Allah be pleased with him) — note: this is Abu Umama al-Harithi, not al-Bahili — that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever usurps the right of a Muslim with a false oath, Allah has made the Fire obligatory for him and has forbidden him Paradise.' A man said: 'Even if it is something trivial, O Messenger of Allah?' He said: 'Even if it is a twig of the arak tree.'
572.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Harithi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) — catatan: ini adalah Abu Umama al-Harithi, bukan al-Bahili — bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa merampas hak seorang Muslim dengan sumpah palsu, Allah telah mewajibkan neraka baginya dan mengharamkan surga atasnya.' Seorang pria bertanya: 'Meskipun sesuatu yang sepele, wahai Rasulullah?' Beliau bersabda: 'Meskipun hanya setangkai pohon siwak.'

573.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not come to prayer while suppressing the urge to relieve yourself. And do not single yourself out in supplication without including those who are with you.'
573.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah kalian mendatangi shalat sementara kalian menahan buang air. Dan janganlah berdoa hanya untuk dirimu sendiri tanpa menyertakan orang-orang yang bersamamu.'

574.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'On Friday, angels stand at the gate of the mosque recording people according to their order of arrival — the first, then the one after him. When the imam sits down, the scrolls are folded and they come to listen to the reminder.'
574.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Pada hari Jumat, para malaikat berdiri di pintu masjid mencatat orang-orang sesuai urutan kedatangan mereka — yang pertama, kemudian yang setelahnya. Ketika imam duduk, lembaran-lembaran dilipat dan mereka datang untuk mendengarkan dzikir.'

575.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Spitting in the mosque is a sin, and its expiation is to bury it.'
575.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Meludah di masjid adalah dosa, dan kafaratnya adalah menguburkannya.'

576.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: 'Barley bread was never left over in the household of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ.'
576.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: 'Roti gandum tidak pernah tersisa di rumah tangga Rasulullah ﷺ.'

577.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Do not pray at the rising of the sun nor at its setting, for it rises between the two horns of Satan, and at that time the disbelievers prostrate to it.'
577.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Janganlah shalat saat matahari terbit dan saat terbenam, karena sesungguhnya ia terbit di antara dua tanduk setan, dan pada saat itu orang-orang kafir bersujud kepadanya.'

578.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ used to pray two rak'ahs after witr while sitting, reciting in them Surah al-Zalzala and Surah al-Kafirun.
578.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ biasa shalat dua rakaat setelah witir sambil duduk, membaca Surah al-Zalzalah dan Surah al-Kafirun di dalamnya.

579.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Four people continue to receive reward after their death: the one who dies while stationed at the frontier in the cause of Allah; the one whose practice is followed and acted upon; the one who gives ongoing charity, as the reward continues as long as the charity is ongoing; and the one who leaves behind a righteous child who supplicates for him.'
579.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Empat orang terus menerima pahala setelah kematian mereka: orang yang mati dalam keadaan berjaga di perbatasan di jalan Allah; orang yang amal perbuatannya diikuti dan diamalkan; orang yang memberikan sedekah jariyah, karena pahalanya terus mengalir selama sedekah tersebut terus berjalan; dan orang yang meninggalkan anak shalih yang mendoakannya.'

580.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him not wear silk or gold.'
580.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa beriman kepada Allah dan Hari Akhir, janganlah ia memakai sutra atau emas.'

581.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day, let him not wear silk or gold.' The narrator said this was reported through two different chains.
581.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa beriman kepada Allah dan Hari Akhir, janganlah ia memakai sutra atau emas.' Perawi berkata bahwa hadits ini diriwayatkan melalui dua sanad yang berbeda.

582.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A man who is not a prophet will intercede on the Day of Resurrection, and his intercession will be accepted. A number of people equal to the tribes of Rabi'a and Mudar will enter Paradise through his intercession.' The people said: 'What about Rabi'a and Mudar?' He said: 'I am only telling you what I was told.'
582.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang pria yang bukan nabi akan memberi syafaat pada Hari Kiamat, dan syafaatnya akan diterima. Sejumlah orang sebanding dengan suku Rabi'ah dan Mudhar akan masuk surga melalui syafaatnya.' Orang-orang bertanya: 'Bagaimana dengan Rabi'ah dan Mudhar?' Beliau bersabda: 'Aku hanya memberitahu kalian apa yang diberitahukan kepadaku.'

583.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever intercedes for his brother and his brother gives him a gift for it and he accepts it — he has come to a great gate of usury.'
583.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa memberi syafaat untuk saudaranya lalu saudaranya memberinya hadiah atas hal itu dan dia menerimanya — maka dia telah mendatangi pintu riba yang besar.'

584.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The one who initiates the greeting of peace is more deserving of Allah and His Messenger.'
584.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Orang yang memulai salam lebih berhak mendapat ridha Allah dan Rasul-Nya.'

585.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Ablution expiates what came before it. Then the prayer becomes supererogatory for the one who performs it.' Abu Umama said: 'He used to repeat this hadith often, so much that we would say: He will not stop until he reaches the point of permitting abandonment of obligatory prayer — but he did not say that.'
585.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Wudhu menghapus dosa-dosa sebelumnya. Kemudian shalat menjadi sunnah bagi yang melakukannya.' Abu Umama berkata: 'Beliau sering mengulang hadits ini, hingga kami berkata: Beliau tidak akan berhenti sampai mengizinkan meninggalkan shalat wajib — namun beliau tidak mengatakan itu.'

586.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Sitting in the place of prayer from the dawn prayer until the sun rises is more beloved to me than freeing four slaves from the children of Isma'il. And similarly after the Asr prayer until the sun sets.'
586.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Duduk di tempat shalat dari shalat Subuh hingga matahari terbit lebih aku cintai daripada membebaskan empat budak dari keturunan Isma'il. Dan begitu pula setelah shalat Asar hingga matahari terbenam.'

587.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Three things are forbidden: do not come to prayer while suppressing the urge to relieve yourself; do not single yourself out in supplication and leave out those with you; and do not look into people's houses without their permission.'
587.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tiga hal dilarang: jangan datang ke shalat sementara kamu menahan buang air; jangan berdoa hanya untuk dirimu dan meninggalkan orang-orang bersamamu; dan jangan mengintip ke dalam rumah orang lain tanpa izin mereka.'

588.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ taught them a supplication after eating: 'All praise is due to Allah who fed us, gave us drink, satisfied us, and gave us all manner of blessings.' He kept repeating it until his companions had memorized it.
588.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ mengajarkan mereka doa setelah makan: 'Segala puji bagi Allah yang telah memberi kami makan, memberi kami minum, mengenyangkan kami, dan memberi kami berbagai macam nikmat.' Beliau terus mengulanginya hingga para sahabatnya menghafalnya.

589.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'I will know every member of my nation on the Day of Resurrection.' His companions said: 'How will you know them, O Messenger of Allah, among the multitudes of creation?' He said: 'Do you not see that if you enter a herd in which there are black horses, you can distinguish a dun horse among them? That is how I will recognize them — they will be bright white from the traces of ablution.'
589.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Aku akan mengenali setiap anggota umatku pada Hari Kiamat.' Para sahabatnya bertanya: 'Bagaimana Anda mengenali mereka, wahai Rasulullah, di tengah banyaknya makhluk?' Beliau bersabda: 'Tidakkah kamu melihat bahwa jika kamu masuk ke dalam kawanan yang ada kuda hitam di dalamnya, kamu bisa membedakan kuda abu-abu di antara mereka? Begitulah aku mengenali mereka — mereka bercahaya putih dari bekas wudhu.'

590.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that he witnessed the Farewell Pilgrimage with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ. He said: 'I was pushing back the people's camels from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, and I was thirty years old at the time. I heard him give the Farewell Sermon.'
590.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia menyaksikan Haji Wada' bersama Rasulullah ﷺ. Dia berkata: 'Aku sedang menjauhkan unta-unta orang dari Rasulullah ﷺ, dan saat itu aku berumur tiga puluh tahun. Aku mendengar beliau menyampaikan khutbah perpisahan.'

591.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Those in whose hearts is deviance — they are the Khawarij. And those whose faces are whitened and those whose faces are blackened — they are the Khawarij as well.' He recited the verse: 'On the Day when some faces are whitened and some faces are blackened.'
591.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Mereka yang dalam hatinya ada penyimpangan — mereka adalah Khawarij. Dan mereka yang wajahnya memutih dan yang wajahnya menghitam — mereka pun adalah Khawarij.' Beliau membacakan ayat: 'Pada hari ketika ada wajah-wajah yang memutih dan wajah-wajah yang menghitam.'

592.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said during the Farewell Pilgrimage: 'Perhaps you will not see me again in this place after today. Therefore, worship your Lord, perform the five prayers, fast Ramadan, pay the zakat on your wealth, and perform the hajj to the House of your Lord. Obey those in authority over you, and you will enter the Paradise of your Lord.'
592.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda pada Haji Wada': 'Mungkin kalian tidak akan melihatku lagi di tempat ini setelah hari ini. Karena itu, sembahlah Rabb kalian, lakukan shalat lima waktu, puasa Ramadan, bayar zakat dari harta kalian, dan lakukan haji ke Baitullah. Taati mereka yang memiliki otoritas atas kalian, niscaya kalian masuk surga Rabb kalian.'

593.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The beginning of my mission was the supplication of Ibrahim, and the glad tidings of Isa. And my mother saw when she gave birth to me a light that illuminated the palaces of Syria.'
593.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Awal misi kenabianku adalah doa Ibrahim, dan kabar gembira dari Isa. Dan ibuku melihat ketika melahirkanku sebuah cahaya yang menerangi istana-istana Syam.'

594.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ commanded the killing of two types of snakes: the one with two white stripes on its back and the dock-tailed one, for they cause miscarriage and damage eyesight. He permitted killing of snakes in general except for the domestic house snake.
594.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ memerintahkan pembunuhan dua jenis ular: yang memiliki dua garis putih di punggungnya dan yang berekor pendek, karena keduanya menyebabkan keguguran dan merusak penglihatan. Beliau mengizinkan pembunuhan ular pada umumnya kecuali ular rumah yang jinak.

595.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Allah and His angels send blessings upon the people of the first row. Straighten your rows, close the gaps, and align your shoulders — for Satan enters the gaps between you like a small lamb.'
595.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Allah dan para malaikat-Nya bershalawat atas orang-orang di shaf pertama. Luruskanlah shaf-shaf kalian, tutuplah celah-celah, dan rapatkan bahu kalian — karena setan masuk ke celah-celah di antara kalian seperti anak kambing kecil.'

596.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Close your doors, cover your vessels, tie your water-skins, and extinguish your lamps — for the shaytan does not open a closed door, uncover a covered vessel, or untie a tied water-skin. And the mouse may set fire to the people of the house.'
596.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tutuplah pintu-pintu kalian, tutuplah bejana-bejana kalian, ikat kantong air kalian, dan padamkan lampu-lampu kalian — karena setan tidak membuka pintu yang tertutup, tidak membuka bejana yang tertutup, dan tidak melepas kantong yang terikat. Dan tikus bisa membakar rumah orang.'

597.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'O son of Adam, if you give generously from your surplus it is good for you, and if you withhold it is bad for you. You are not blamed for having enough. Begin with those you are responsible to support. The upper hand is better than the lower hand.'
597.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Wahai anak Adam, jika kamu berinfak dari kelebihanmu itu baik bagimu, dan jika kamu menahannya itu buruk bagimu. Kamu tidak dicela karena memiliki kecukupan. Mulailah dengan orang-orang yang menjadi tanggunganmu. Tangan yang di atas lebih baik dari tangan yang di bawah.'

598.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came to the Messenger of Allah ﷺ and said: 'O Messenger of Allah, I have committed something that requires a prescribed punishment — apply it to me.' The Prophet ﷺ did not question him about it. Then the prayer time came and the man prayed with the Prophet ﷺ. When the Prophet finished, the man again said: 'Apply the prescribed punishment to me.' The Prophet said: 'Did you not pray with us?' He said: 'Yes.' He said: 'You are forgiven.'
598.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang pria datang kepada Rasulullah ﷺ dan berkata: 'Wahai Rasulullah, aku telah melakukan sesuatu yang mengharuskan had — tegakkanlah atasku.' Nabi ﷺ tidak menanyainya tentang hal itu. Kemudian waktu shalat tiba dan pria itu shalat bersama Nabi ﷺ. Setelah Nabi selesai, pria itu kembali berkata: 'Tegakkanlah had atasku.' Nabi bersabda: 'Bukankah kamu sudah shalat bersama kami?' Dia berkata: 'Ya.' Beliau bersabda: 'Kamu diampuni.'

599.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever performs ablution well, then washes his face — every sin his face committed flows off with the water. Then he washes his right hand — every sin his right hand committed flows off with the water. Then his left hand — likewise. Then he wipes his head. Then he washes his right foot — every sin his foot walked toward flows off. Then his left foot — likewise. Then he stands and prays, seeking Allah's face — and he emerges from his sins as on the day his mother bore him. And if he stands to pray, Allah raises his degree with each prostration and removes a sin.'
599.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa berwudhu dengan baik, lalu membasuh wajahnya — setiap dosa yang dilakukan wajahnya mengalir bersama air. Kemudian membasuh tangan kanannya — setiap dosa yang dilakukan tangan kanannya mengalir bersama air. Kemudian tangan kirinya — begitu pula. Kemudian mengusap kepalanya. Kemudian membasuh kaki kanannya — setiap dosa yang dilangkah kakinya mengalir. Kemudian kaki kirinya — begitu pula. Kemudian dia berdiri dan shalat, mencari wajah Allah — dan dia keluar dari dosa-dosanya seperti pada hari ibunya melahirkannya. Dan jika dia berdiri untuk shalat, Allah mengangkat derajatnya dengan setiap sujud dan menghapus satu dosa.'

600.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umama al-Bahili (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The angels stand at the gate of the mosque on Friday with scrolls, recording people by their order of arrival — the first, then the one after him. When the imam comes out and sits on the pulpit, the scrolls are folded. Those who came early receive the full reward of a year's fasting and night prayer for their early arrival. Those who came after the scrolls were folded still attain the Jumu'ah but are not written in the scrolls of those who came early.'
600.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umama al-Bahili (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Para malaikat berdiri di pintu masjid pada hari Jumat dengan lembaran-lembaran catatan, mencatat orang-orang sesuai urutan kedatangan mereka — yang pertama, kemudian yang setelahnya. Ketika imam keluar dan duduk di mimbar, lembaran-lembaran dilipat. Mereka yang datang lebih awal mendapat pahala penuh puasa dan shalat malam setahun atas kedatangan awal mereka. Mereka yang datang setelah lembaran dilipat tetap mendapatkan Jumu'ah namun tidak tercatat dalam lembaran mereka yang datang lebih awal.'
